QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_40

question.text: Trading Member (TM) level position limits for individual stock derivatives (combined futures and options) are:

answer.text: 20% of the applicable Market Wide Position Limit (MWPL) per Exchange
15% of the market-wide position limit or Rs. 50 crore, whichever is higher
Equal to the market-wide position limit
Fixed at 5% of free-float market cap

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For individual stock derivatives, the TM-level combined futures and options position limit is 20% of the applicable MWPL per Exchange. The Clearing Corporation specifies this on the last trading day of each month for the next month. For index derivatives, TM limits are different: higher of Rs. 7,500 crore or 15% of total OI.
**The Trap:** 15% appears in TM limits for INDEX derivatives, not stock derivatives. Stock TM limit is 20% of MWPL. Don't confuse index and stock limits.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Position limit hierarchy for stocks: Client (higher of 1% free float or 5% OI) < TM (20% of MWPL) < MWPL (20% of free float).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Stock TM limit = 20% of MWPL. Index TM limit = higher of Rs. 7,500 Cr or 15% OI. Remember: stocks use MWPL percentages, indices use absolute values.
